lines.english: Lesson 24
lines.chinese: 第24课
lines.raw: Lesson 24 | 第24课

lines.english: A skeleton in the cupboard
lines.chinese: 橱柜里的骷髅
lines.raw: A skeleton in the cupboard | 橱柜里的骷髅

lines.english: Who was Sebastian?
lines.chinese: 塞巴斯蒂安是谁？
lines.raw: Who was Sebastian? | 塞巴斯蒂安是谁？

lines.english: We often read in novels how a seemingly respectable person
lines.chinese: 我们经常在小说中读到，一个看似体面的人
lines.raw: We often read in novels how a seemingly respectable person | 我们经常在小说中读到，一个看似体面的人

lines.english: or family has some terrible secret which has been concealed from strangers for years.
lines.chinese: 或家庭隐藏着某个可怕的秘密，多年来一直对陌生人保密。
lines.raw: or family has some terrible secret which has been concealed from strangers for years. | 或家庭隐藏着某个可怕的秘密，多年来一直对陌生人保密。

lines.english: The English language possesses a vivid saying to describe this sort of situation.
lines.chinese: 英语中有一个生动的说法来描述这种情况。
lines.raw: The English language possesses a vivid saying to describe this sort of situation. | 英语中有一个生动的说法来描述这种情况。

lines.english: The terrible secret is called 'a skeleton in the cupboard'.
lines.chinese: 这个可怕的秘密被称为“橱柜里的骷髅”。
lines.raw: The terrible secret is called 'a skeleton in the cupboard'. | 这个可怕的秘密被称为“橱柜里的骷髅”。

lines.english: At some dramatic moment in the story, the terrible secret becomes known and a reputation is ruined.
lines.chinese: 在故事的某个戏剧性时刻，这个可怕的秘密公诸于世，声誉也随之毁掉。
lines.raw: At some dramatic moment in the story, the terrible secret becomes known and a reputation is ruined. | 在故事的某个戏剧性时刻，这个可怕的秘密公诸于世，声誉也随之毁掉。

lines.english: The reader's hair stands on end when he reads in the final pages of the novel that the heroine,
lines.chinese: 当读者在小说的最后几页读到女主人公——
lines.raw: The reader's hair stands on end when he reads in the final pages of the novel that the heroine, | 当读者在小说的最后几页读到女主人公——

lines.english: a dear old lady who had always been so kind to everybody, had, in her youth, poisoned every one of her five husbands.
lines.chinese: 一位一直对每个人都非常和蔼可亲的老太太，在年轻时毒死了她的五个丈夫时，会毛骨悚然。
lines.raw: a dear old lady who had always been so kind to everybody, had, in her youth, poisoned every one of her five husbands. | 一位一直对每个人都非常和蔼可亲的老太太，在年轻时毒死了她的五个丈夫时，会毛骨悚然。

lines.english: It is all very well for such things to occur in fiction.
lines.chinese: 这种事情发生在小说里是完全可以的。
lines.raw: It is all very well for such things to occur in fiction. | 这种事情发生在小说里是完全可以的。

lines.english: To varying degrees, we all have secrets which we do not want even our closest friends to learn,
lines.chinese: 在不同程度上，我们都有一些甚至不想让最亲密的朋友知道的秘密，
lines.raw: To varying degrees, we all have secrets which we do not want even our closest friends to learn, | 在不同程度上，我们都有一些甚至不想让最亲密的朋友知道的秘密，

lines.english: but few of us have skeletons in the cupboard.
lines.chinese: 但我们中很少有人有“橱柜里的骷髅”。
lines.raw: but few of us have skeletons in the cupboard. | 但我们中很少有人有“橱柜里的骷髅”。

lines.english: The only person I know who has a skeleton in the cupboard is George Carlton, and he is very proud of the fact.
lines.chinese: 我认识的唯一一个有“橱柜里的骷髅”的人是乔治·卡尔顿，他对此引以为豪。
lines.raw: The only person I know who has a skeleton in the cupboard is George Carlton, and he is very proud of the fact. | 我认识的唯一一个有“橱柜里的骷髅”的人是乔治·卡尔顿，他对此引以为豪。

lines.english: George studied medicine in his youth.
lines.chinese: 乔治年轻时学医。
lines.raw: George studied medicine in his youth. | 乔治年轻时学医。

lines.english: Instead of becoming a doctor, however, he became a successful writer of detective stories.
lines.chinese: 然而，他没有成为一名医生，而是成为了一名成功的侦探小说作家。
lines.raw: Instead of becoming a doctor, however, he became a successful writer of detective stories. | 然而，他没有成为一名医生，而是成为了一名成功的侦探小说作家。

lines.english: I once spent an uncomfortable weekend which I shall never forget at his house.
lines.chinese: 我有一次在他家度过了一个令我永生难忘的不舒服的周末。
lines.raw: I once spent an uncomfortable weekend which I shall never forget at his house. | 我有一次在他家度过了一个令我永生难忘的不舒服的周末。

lines.english: George showed me to the guestroom which, he said, was rarely used.
lines.chinese: 乔治把我带到客房，他说那个房间很少用。
lines.raw: George showed me to the guestroom which, he said, was rarely used. | 乔治把我带到客房，他说那个房间很少用。

lines.english: He told me to unpack my things and then come down to dinner.
lines.chinese: 他让我把行李打开，然后下来吃饭。
lines.raw: He told me to unpack my things and then come down to dinner. | 他让我把行李打开，然后下来吃饭。

lines.english: After I had stacked my shirts and underclothes in two empty drawers, I decided to hang one of the two suits I had brought with me in the cupboard.
lines.chinese: 我把衬衫和内衣叠放在两个空抽屉里后，决定把我带来的两套西装中的一套挂在橱柜里。
lines.raw: After I had stacked my shirts and underclothes in two empty drawers, I decided to hang one of the two suits I had brought with me in the cupboard. | 我把衬衫和内衣叠放在两个空抽屉里后，决定把我带来的两套西装中的一套挂在橱柜里。

lines.english: I opened the cupboard door and then stood in front of it petrified.
lines.chinese: 我打开橱柜门，然后僵在原地，吓呆了。
lines.raw: I opened the cupboard door and then stood in front of it petrified. | 我打开橱柜门，然后僵在原地，吓呆了。

lines.english: A skeleton was dangling before my eyes.
lines.chinese: 一个骷髅在我眼前晃动。
lines.raw: A skeleton was dangling before my eyes. | 一个骷髅在我眼前晃动。

lines.english: The sudden movement of the door made it sway slightly and it gave me the impression that it was about to leap out at me.
lines.chinese: 门的突然移动使它轻轻摇晃，给我一种它要向我扑过来的感觉。
lines.raw: The sudden movement of the door made it sway slightly and it gave me the impression that it was about to leap out at me. | 门的突然移动使它轻轻摇晃，给我一种它要向我扑过来的感觉。

lines.english: Dropping my suit, I dashed downstairs to tell George.
lines.chinese: 我扔下西装，冲下楼去告诉乔治。
lines.raw: Dropping my suit, I dashed downstairs to tell George. | 我扔下西装，冲下楼去告诉乔治。

lines.english: This was worse than 'a terrible secret'; this was a real skeleton!
lines.chinese: 这比“可怕的秘密”更糟糕；这是一个真正的骷髅！
lines.raw: This was worse than 'a terrible secret'; this was a real skeleton! | 这比“可怕的秘密”更糟糕；这是一个真正的骷髅！

lines.english: But George was unsympathetic.
lines.chinese: 但乔治无动于衷。
lines.raw: But George was unsympathetic. | 但乔治无动于衷。

lines.english: 'Oh, that,' he said with a smile as if he were talking about an old friend.
lines.chinese: “哦，那个啊，”他微笑着说，好像在谈论一位老朋友。
lines.raw: 'Oh, that,' he said with a smile as if he were talking about an old friend. | “哦，那个啊，”他微笑着说，好像在谈论一位老朋友。

lines.english: 'That's Sebastian. You forget that I was a medical student once upon a time.'
lines.chinese: “那是塞巴斯蒂安。你忘了我以前也是个医学院的学生。”
lines.raw: 'That's Sebastian. You forget that I was a medical student once upon a time.' | “那是塞巴斯蒂安。你忘了我以前也是个医学院的学生。”
